top_categories.workout_context: UCI Gran Fondo Ireland's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Race-pace efforts that mimic UCI Gran Fondo Ireland's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
UCI Gran Fondo Ireland's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 95 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of UCI Gran Fondo Ireland. Train it until it feels boring.
At UCI Gran Fondo Ireland, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
At mile 60 of 95, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At UCI Gran Fondo Ireland, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 95 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 3,858ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for UCI Gran Fondo Ireland’s 95 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at UCI Gran Fondo Ireland requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, Race Simulation, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 95 miles of mixed terrain in Sligo, Ireland.
